name: justext
documentation:
# jusText ```{image} https://api.travis-ci.org/miso-belica/jusText.png?branch=master :target: https://travis-ci.org/miso-belica/jusText ``` Program jusText is a tool for removing boilerplate content, such as navigation links, headers, and footers from HTML pages. It is [designed](doc/algorithm.rst) to preserve mainly text containing full sentences and it is therefore well suited for creating linguistic resources such as Web corpora. You can [try it online](http://nlp.fi.muni.cz/projects/justext/). This is a fork of original (currently unmaintained) code of [jusText] hosted on Google Code. Adaptations of the algorithm to other languages: - [C++](https://github.com/endredy/jusText) - [Go](https://github.com/JalfResi/justext) - [Java](https://github.com/wizenoze/justext-java) Some libraries using jusText: - [chirp](https://github.com/9b/chirp) - [lazynlp](https://github.com/chiphuyen/lazynlp) - [off-topic-memento-toolkit](https://github.com/oduwsdl/off-topic-memento-toolkit) - [pears](https://github.com/PeARSearch/PeARS-orchard) - [readability calculator](https://github.com/joaopalotti/readability_calculator) - [sky](https://github.com/kootenpv/sky) Some currently (Jan 2020) maintained alternatives: - [dragnet](https://github.com/dragnet-org/dragnet) - [html2text](https://github.com/Alir3z4/html2text) - [inscriptis](https://github.com/weblyzard/inscriptis) - [newspaper](https://github.com/codelucas/newspaper) - [python-readability](https://github.com/buriy/python-readability) - [trafilatura](https://github.com/adbar/trafilatura) ## Installation Make sure you have [Python] 2.7+/3.5+ and [pip](https://pip.pypa.io/en/stable/) ([Windows](http://docs.python-guide.org/en/latest/starting/install/win/), [Linux](http://docs.python-guide.org/en/latest/starting/install/linux/)) installed. Run simply: ```bash $ [sudo] pip install justext ``` ## Dependencies ``` lxml (version depends on your Python version) ``` ## Usage ```bash $ python -m justext -s Czech -o text.txt http://www.zdrojak.cz/clanky/automaticke-zabezpeceni/ $ python -m justext -s English -o plain_text.txt english_page.html $ python -m justext --help # for more info ``` ## Python API ```python import requests import justext response = requests.get("http://planet.python.org/") paragraphs = justext.justext(response.content, justext.get_stoplist("English")) for paragraph in paragraphs: if not paragraph.is_boilerplate: print paragraph.text ``` ## Testing Run tests via ```bash $ py.test-2.7 && py.test-3.5 && py.test-3.6 && py.test-3.7 && py.test-3.8 && py.test-3.9 ``` ## Acknowledgements This software has been developed at the [Natural Language Processing Centre] of [Masaryk University in Brno] with a financial support from [PRESEMT] and [Lexical Computing Ltd.] It also relates to [PhD research] of Jan Pomikálek. % :changelog: # Changelog for jusText ## 3.0.1 (2024-05-09) - *BUG FIX:* Fix issue with new version of lxml [#48](https://github.com/miso-belica/jusText/pull/48). ## 3.0.0 (2021-10-21) - *INCOMPATIBLE CHANGE:* Dropped support for Python 3.4 and below. - *BUG FIX:* Don't join words separated only by `
` tag. - *BUG FIX:* List available stop-lists alphabetically. ## 2.2.0 (2016-03-06) - *INCOMPATIBLE CHANGE:* Stop words are case insensitive. - *INCOMPATIBLE CHANGE:* Dropped support for Python 3.2 - *BUG FIX:* Preserve new lines from original text in paragraphs. ## 2.1.1 (2014-05-27) - *BUG FIX:* Function `decode_html` now respects parameter `errors` when falling to `default_encoding` [#9](https://github.com/miso-belica/jusText/issues/9). ## 2.1.0 (2014-01-25) - *FEATURE:* Added XPath selector to the paragrahs. XPath selector is also available in detailed output as `xpath` attribute of `

` tag [#5](https://github.com/miso-belica/jusText/pull/5). ## 2.0.0 (2013-08-26) - *FEATURE:* Added pluggable DOM preprocessor. - *FEATURE:* Added support for Python 3.2+. - *INCOMPATIBLE CHANGE:* Paragraphs are instances of `justext.paragraph.Paragraph`. - *INCOMPATIBLE CHANGE:* Script 'justext' removed in favour of command `python -m justext`. - *FEATURE:* It's possible to enter an URI as input document in CLI. - *FEATURE:* It is possible to pass unicode string directly. ## 1.2.0 (2011-08-08) - *FEATURE:* Character counts used instead of word counts where possible in order to make the algorithm work well in the language independent mode (without a stoplist) for languages where counting words is not easy (Japanese, Chinese, Thai, etc). - *BUG FIX:* More robust parsing of meta tags containing the information about used charset. - *BUG FIX:* Corrected decoding of HTML entities \€ to \Ÿ ## 1.1.0 (2011-03-09) - First public release. [justext]: http://code.google.com/p/justext/ [lexical computing ltd.]: http://lexicalcomputing.com/ [lxml]: http://lxml.de/ [masaryk university in brno]: http://nlp.fi.muni.cz/en [natural language processing centre]: http://nlp.fi.muni.cz/en/nlpc [phd research]: http://is.muni.cz/th/45523/fi_d/phdthesis.pdf [presemt]: http://presemt.eu/ [python]: http://www.python.org/

modules.name: justext.utils
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: justext.utils.normalize_whitespace
documentation:
Translates multiple whitespace into single space character.
If there is at least one new line character chunk is replaced
by single LF (Unix new line) character.

functions.name: justext.utils._replace_whitespace
documentation: Normalize all spacing characters that aren't a newline to a space.

functions.name: justext.utils.is_blank
documentation:
Returns `True` if string contains only white-space characters
or is empty. Otherwise `False` is returned.

functions.name: justext.utils.get_stoplists
documentation: Returns a collection of built-in stop-lists.

functions.name: justext.utils.get_stoplist
documentation: Returns an built-in stop-list for the language as a set of words.

modules.name: justext.paragraph
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: justext.paragraph.Paragraph
documentation: Object representing one block of text in HTML.

modules.name: justext._compat
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

modules.name: justext.__main__
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: justext.__main__.output_default
documentation:
Outputs the paragraphs as: text of the first paragraph text of the second paragraph ... where is

, or which indicates standard paragraph, heading or boilerplate respecitvely.

functions.name: justext.__main__.output_detailed
documentation:
Same as output_default, but only

tags are used and the following attributes are added: class, cfclass and heading.

functions.name: justext.__main__.output_krdwrd
documentation:
Outputs the paragraphs in a KrdWrd compatible format:
class<TAB>first text node
class<TAB>second text node
...
where class is 1, 2 or 3 which means
boilerplate, undecided or good respectively. Headings are output as
undecided.

documentation:

-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: justext.core.html_to_dom
documentation: Converts HTML to DOM.

functions.name: justext.core.decode_html
documentation:
Converts a `html` containing an HTML page into Unicode.
Tries to guess character encoding from meta tag.

functions.name: justext.core.get_prev_neighbour
documentation:
Return the class of the paragraph at the top end of the short/neargood
paragraphs block. If ignore_neargood is True, than only 'bad' or 'good'
can be returned, otherwise 'neargood' can be returned, too.

functions.name: justext.core.get_next_neighbour
documentation:
Return the class of the paragraph at the bottom end of the short/neargood
paragraphs block. If ignore_neargood is True, than only 'bad' or 'good'
can be returned, otherwise 'neargood' can be returned, too.

functions.name: justext.core.revise_paragraph_classification
documentation:
Context-sensitive paragraph classification. Assumes that classify_pragraphs
has already been called.

functions.name: justext.core.justext
documentation:
Converts an HTML page into a list of classified paragraphs. Each paragraph
is represented as instance of class ˙˙justext.paragraph.Paragraph˙˙.

classes.name: justext.core.ParagraphMaker

methods.name: justext.core.ParagraphMaker.make_paragraphs
documentation: Converts DOM into paragraphs.

A class for converting a HTML page represented as a DOM object into a list
of paragraphs.
super(...).__init__() breaks Python 2.7 - TypeError: super() argument 1 must be type, not classobj
